narrative_hint: The execution plan confirms a TABLE ACCESS FULL on the ORDERS table — 982,000 rows estimated, cost 87,800. The predicates are CUSTOMER_ID and STATUS but no composite index exists covering both. Oracle is doing a full scan of 982K rows to find the matching subset. The fix is a composite index on (CUSTOMER_ID, STATUS) or (CUSTOMER_ID, STATUS, CREATED_AT) if the query also filters by date. Claude should provide a specific CREATE INDEX statement and note the urgency given current active sessions.
